title: lycopodiella lateralis
intro: Lycopodiella lateralis, also known as the lateral club moss, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the family Lycopodiaceae, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Lycopodiella lateralis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat wounds, making it a valuable herb in folk medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although its efficacy and safety as a medicinal plant require further research and validation.
